expect: Of last 50 sessions, ≥5 will show multi-layer cascade failures (temporal→quality or quality→attention). Null: failures are isolated — cascade rate <10%.
actual: 5 cascade events; 50/50 sessions (100%) had ≥1 active cascade; 31/50 (62%) had ≥1 3-layer cascade. Null firmly rejected.
diff: Expected: ≥5 cascade sessions (10%). Actual: 50 sessions (100%). Delta: +40x. Cascades are background state, not exceptional failures. Independence assumption in L-1005 compound FNR formula is WRONG.

cascades.description: check_observer_staleness() regex bug: 0 firings S398-S425. 7 stale baselines (mean 125s old) undetected.

F-CON1 baseline hardcoded S189 (240s stale). maintenance_drift Layer 3 generated false URGENT C1-conflict across all 50 sessions.
orient_checks.py 3 false positives from numeric-value pattern matching. S427-S429 chased non-issues.

implications: Cascades are default operating state, not exceptional failures
L-1005 compound FNR formula assumes independent filters — independence is wrong when cascades are 100%
Actual compound FNR exceeds the independent-filter calculation
Need cascade-aware monitoring (cross-layer coordination), not just per-layer optimization
Confirmation bias acknowledged: UCB1 dispatched to filtering after L-1005 made cascade evidence salient
